# Step 2 — Create Purchase Request Form

> The form where the Creator builds a new Purchase Request by selecting a PR type, writing a description, and adding line items. The PR is saved as **Draft** until submitted.

![Create PR Form — Empty](data:image/png;base64,)

---

## 2.1 Access Path

1. PR List → **Add Request** → **Blank PR** or **PR Template**
2. URL on arrival: `/procurement/purchase-request/new`

---

## 2.1a Purpose & Use Cases

| Creation Mode | Purpose | Use Case |
|---------------|---------|----------|
| **Blank PR** | Create a brand-new purchase request from scratch | Use when the order is unique, ad-hoc, or does not match any saved template |
| **PR from Template** | Pre-fill the form with a saved set of items and settings | Use when ordering the same products regularly (e.g. weekly kitchen supplies) to reduce data-entry time and avoid mistakes |

---

## 2.2 Flow Position

```mermaid
graph TD
    list["1. PR List"] --> create["2. Create PR"]
    list --> tmpl["3. Create from Template"]
    create --> detail["4. PR Detail"]
    tmpl --> detail
    detail -->|Edit| edit["5. Edit PR"]
    edit --> submit["6. Submit Confirmation"]
    submit --> inprog(["Submitted - In Progress"])
    inprog -->|Returned| returned["7. Returned PR"]
    returned --> edit
    detail -->|Delete| del["8. Delete Confirmation"]
    del --> list
    style create fill:#1a56db,color:#fff,stroke:#1a56db
```

---

## 2.3 Auto-Populated Header Fields

These fields are read-only and set automatically from the logged-in user's profile. The Creator cannot change them.

| Field | Description | Source | Default Value | Example |
|-------|-------------|--------|---------------|---------|
| **Date** | The date the PR form was opened; used as the official PR creation date | Server date on form open | Today's date (server time, GMT+7) | 2026-04-10 |
| **Requestor** | Full name of the person creating this PR; used for accountability and approval routing | Logged-in user's display name | Logged-in user's full name | สมชาย ใจดี |
| **Department** | The department the requestor belongs to; used for budget tracking and approval chain selection | User's primary department | User's assigned primary department | Guest Service and Bell Staff |

---

## 2.4 Editable Header Fields

![Workflow Type Dropdown](data:image/png;base64,)

| Field | Description | Mandatory | Type | Default Value | Validation | Business Logic |
|-------|-------------|-----------|------|---------------|------------|----------------|
| **Workflow** (PR Type) | Determines the approval chain and purchase category. General = standard items; Asset = capital equipment; Market List = daily market purchases. | **Y** | Dropdown | — (blank, must be selected) | Must select before Submit is enabled | Options: **General**, **Asset**, **Market List**. Drives approval routing. |
| **Description** | A short explanation of why this PR is being raised (e.g. "Monthly kitchen supplies for F&B"). Visible to all approvers. | N | Textarea | — (blank) | Max length TBC | Free-text purpose/note for the PR |

> ⚠️ **Discrepancy:** The field label in the UI is **"Workflow\*"** but the dropdown options (General, Asset, Market List) are PR *types*, not workflow names. The BRD refers to this as "PR type selection". This label is misleading — "PR Type" would be more accurate. Flag for dev team.

---

## 2.5 Items Section

![Items Section — Empty](data:image/png;base64,)

When no items exist, the section shows:
> "No items added yet. Start by adding items to this purchase request."  
> with an **Add Item** button centred in the empty area.

### Adding a Line Item

Clicking **Add Item** inserts an inline editable row directly in the items grid:

![Item Row — Added](data:image/png;base64,)

| Column | Description | Mandatory | Type | Default Value | Validation | Business Logic |
|--------|-------------|-----------|------|---------------|------------|----------------|
| **Location** | Where this item will be used or stored within the property (e.g. Main Kitchen, Housekeeping Store). Used for delivery routing. | Y | Dropdown (combobox) | Last used location | Location is not empty | Sub-location within the property |
| **Product** | The item being requested. Search by product name or code to select from the inventory master list. | Y | Dropdown (combobox + search) | — (blank) | Product is not empty | Search by product name or code; shows matching inventory items |
| **Requested** | The number of units being requested. Supports up to 3 decimal places for weighted items (e.g. 12.500 kg). | Y | Number | — (blank) | Qty is more than 0; up to 3 decimal places | Requested quantity |
| **Amount** | Total cost for this line = Quantity × Unit Price. Calculated automatically — cannot be edited manually. | Auto | Calculated | 0.00 | Read-only | Quantity × Unit Price (populated after product selected) |
| **Currency** | The currency used for this line item's pricing. Defaults to the property's base currency. | Auto | Read-only | Base currency (THB) | — | Defaults to property base currency (THB in this instance) |
| **Delivery Point** | The specific room, store, or area within the property where the item should be delivered. Optional. | N | Dropdown (lookup) | Last used delivery point for selected Location | — | Options filtered by selected Location; defaults to last used delivery point for that Location |
| **Delivery Date** | The date by which the item is needed. Must not be in the past. Optional — leave blank if no specific date is required. | N | Date picker | — (blank) | Date is today or later | Requested delivery date for this line item |
| **Comment** | Any additional notes for this specific line item (e.g. "must be halal certified"). Visible to purchasers and approvers. | N | Text input | — (blank) | — | Item-level note (visible below the row) |
| **Delete (×)** | Removes this item row from the PR immediately. No confirmation dialog. Cannot be undone once saved. | — | Icon button | — | — | Removes this row immediately (no confirmation) |

Multiple rows can be added by clicking **Add Item** again for each additional product.

---

## 2.6 Action Buttons, Behaviour & Validation

This section covers all action buttons on the Create PR form: their purpose, when they are available, how they behave, and what is validated when activated.

> **Form states:**
> - **New** (URL: `/purchase-request/new`) — before the first successful save with items
> - **Draft saved** (URL: `/purchase-request/:uuid`) — after first save with ≥ 1 item

### Button Summary

| Button | Color | Available: New state | Available: Draft-saved state | Purpose / Use Case |
|--------|-------|---------------------|-----------------------------|--------------------|
| **Cancel** | Outline/Grey | ✅ Always | ✅ Always | Return to PR List without saving — use when you no longer need this PR |
| **Save** | Secondary | ✅ Always | ✅ Always | Save the form as Draft so you can come back later to complete or edit it |
| **Submit** | Blue/Primary | Only when ≥ 1 item added | Only when ≥ 1 item added | Send the completed PR for approval — triggers workflow routing to the first approver |
| **Delete** | Red/Danger | ❌ Not available | ✅ After first save | Permanently remove a Draft PR that is no longer needed |

---

### On Cancel

**Purpose / Use Case:** Abandon the current form and return to the PR List. No data is saved. Use when you opened the form by mistake or decided not to raise a PR.

| # | Check | BR | Pass — what happens | Fail — what happens |
|---|-------|----|---------------------|---------------------|
| 1 | Unsaved changes exist on the form | BR-14 | **Expected:** "Discard changes?" confirmation dialog is shown | **Observed:** Returns to PR List immediately — no warning shown (data is lost silently) |

> ⚠️ **Discrepancy (BR-14):** BRD FR-PR-011 requires a warning when navigating away with unsaved changes. Not observed on the Create form.

---

### On Save

**Purpose / Use Case:** Save progress on the PR as a Draft without submitting. Use when you need to pause mid-entry and return later, or when you want to review item prices before sending for approval.

| # | Check | BR | Pass — what happens | Fail — what happens |
|---|-------|----|---------------------|---------------------|
| 1 | At least one item row is present | BR-13 | URL changes to `/purchase-request/:uuid`; Delete button becomes available | Header is saved only; URL stays at `/new`; no feedback shown |
| 2 | Qty is more than 0 on all item rows | BR-06 | Row is accepted and saved | Row-level error highlight shown on Qty field |
| 3 | Qty has at most 3 decimal places | BR-06 | Value is saved as entered | Value may be rounded silently or rejected (TBC) |
| 4 | Delivery date is today or later (if entered) | BR-10 | Date is accepted | Error shown below the date field |
| 5 | Location is not empty (if row exists) | FR-PR-002 | Row is accepted | Location field highlighted as required |
| 6 | Product is not empty (if row exists) | FR-PR-002 | Unit price and Amount auto-filled | Product field highlighted as required |

> **Note:** Save does not hard-block on item validation — a header-only save (with no items) always succeeds silently. Item-level checks apply only to rows that already exist.

> ⚠️ **Discrepancy:** Saving with no items gives no feedback to the user. Recommend a toast: "Header saved. Add items to create a submittable PR."

**What changes after a successful Save with items:**
- URL becomes `/purchase-request/:uuid` (permanent Draft PR link)
- Status is set to **DRAFT**
- The form remains fully editable
- **Delete** button appears alongside Save and Submit

---

### On Submit

**Purpose / Use Case:** Send the fully prepared PR for approval. Use only when all items are confirmed and the PR is ready to be reviewed by the approver. Submission cannot be undone.

| # | Check | BR | Pass — what happens | Fail — what happens |
|---|-------|----|---------------------|---------------------|
| 1 | Workflow (PR Type) is selected | BR-04 | Submit button is active | Button is disabled; no tooltip explaining why |
| 2 | At least 1 item row is present | BR-05 | Submit button is active | Button is disabled |
| 3 | Location is not empty on all rows | FR-PR-002 | Proceeds to confirmation dialog | Row-level error shown on the Location field |
| 4 | Product is not empty on all rows | FR-PR-002 | Proceeds to confirmation dialog | Row-level error shown on the Product field |
| 5 | Qty is more than 0 on all rows | BR-06 | Proceeds to confirmation dialog | Row-level error shown on the Qty field |
| 6 | Budget is not exceeded | BR-18 | Proceeds (or shows warning depending on org setting) | Warning toast shown, or submission blocked depending on org config |
| 7 | PR status is still DRAFT at time of submit | FR-PR-006 | Submission allowed | Server error returned if another session changed the status |

> ⚠️ **Discrepancy:** No tooltip or inline message explains why the Submit button is disabled. Recommend: disabled-state tooltip "Add at least one item and select a Workflow to submit."

**Confirmation Dialog (shown when all checks pass):**

| Element | Content |
|---------|----------|
| Title | "Submit Purchase Request" |
| Message | "Are you sure you want to submit this purchase request? Once submitted, it will be routed for approval and cannot be edited." |
| **OK** button (Blue/Primary) | Confirms submission — PR status changes `DRAFT` → `INPROGRESS`; Creator redirected to PR Detail (read-only) |
| **Cancel** button (Outline/Grey) | Closes dialog — PR remains in DRAFT; no changes made |

→ For the full confirmation step details, see **[step-06-submit-confirmation.md](step-06-submit-confirmation.md)**

---

### On Delete

**Purpose / Use Case:** Permanently remove a Draft PR that is no longer needed — for example, if the request was raised by mistake or is a duplicate. Only available after the first save (when a UUID exists).

| # | Check | BR | Pass — what happens | Fail — what happens |
|---|-------|----|---------------------|---------------------|
| 1 | PR status is DRAFT | FR-PR-006 | Delete confirmation dialog shown | Delete button not visible |
| 2 | User confirms deletion | BR-14 | PR permanently deleted; redirected to PR List | PR kept; dialog closed with no action |

**Confirmation Dialog:**

| Element | Content |
|---------|----------|
| Title | "Delete Purchase Request" |
| Message | "Are you sure you want to delete this purchase request? This action cannot be undone." |
| **Delete** button (Red/Danger) | Permanently deletes PR and all items — redirected to PR List |
| **Cancel** button (Outline/Grey) | Closes dialog — PR unchanged |

> **Note:** Delete is only available once the PR has been saved with ≥ 1 item and a UUID has been assigned. It cannot be used on an unsaved (New) form.

---

## 2.7 Edge Cases & Error States

| # | Scenario | System Behaviour |
|---|----------|-----------------|
| 1 | Submit clicked with no Workflow selected | Submit button is disabled; no action |
| 2 | Submit clicked with no items | Submit button is disabled; no action |
| 3 | Cancel clicked with unsaved changes | Returns to PR List (no confirmation observed — potential data loss) |
| 4 | Product search returns no results | Empty dropdown state with "No results" (TBC) |
| 5 | Network error during Save | Error toast expected (exact message TBC) |
| 6 | Session expires while filling form | Redirect to `/login`; form data lost |

---

## 2.8 Business Rules

| # | Rule | Source |
|---|------|--------|
| BR-01 | PR reference numbers are auto-generated in format `PR-[YYMM]-[NNNN]` (e.g. PR-2604-0001). System assigns on first Save. | FR-PR-001 |
| BR-02 | Requestor field auto-populates from the logged-in user's profile and **cannot be changed**. | FR-PR-001 |
| BR-03 | Department and Location default to the user's primary department/location assignment. | FR-PR-001 |
| BR-04 | PR Type (Workflow field) drives approval routing. General, Market List, and Asset may follow different approval chains. | FR-PR-001 |
| BR-05 | A PR **must have at least one item** before it can be submitted. Submit button is disabled until ≥ 1 item exists. | FR-PR-002 |
| BR-06 | Quantity supports up to **3 decimal places** (e.g. 12.500 kg). | FR-PR-002 |
| BR-07 | Price supports up to **2 decimal places** (e.g. 15.50). | FR-PR-002 |
| BR-08 | Item subtotal = `Round(Quantity × Unit Price, 2)` — calculated automatically on quantity or price change. | FR-PR-003 |
| BR-09 | Discount amount = `Round(Subtotal × Discount Rate, 2)`. Net amount = `Round(Subtotal - Discount, 2)`. | FR-PR-003 |
| BR-10 | Tax amount = `Round(Net Amount × Tax Rate, 2)`. Item total = `Round(Net Amount + Tax, 2)`. All rounding uses half-up (banker's) rounding. | FR-PR-003 |
| BR-11 | All monetary amounts display with 2 decimal places. Exchange rates display with 5 decimal places. | FR-PR-003 |
| BR-12 | Real-time calculation: totals update as the user enters quantity or price. | FR-PR-003 |
| BR-13 | Draft PRs can be **saved without submission** at any time for later completion. | FR-PR-001 |
| BR-14 | Navigating away from an unsaved form should show a **warning message** (browser or in-app). Currently not observed in UI. | FR-PR-011 |
| BR-15 | Auto-save every 30 seconds once a Draft UUID exists. | FR-PR-011 |
| BR-16 | Vendor suggestions appear based on best price for similar items purchased in the **last 90 days**. | FR-PR-002 |
| BR-17 | Supported attachment types: PDF, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX, JPG, PNG, CSV. Max 10 MB per file; 50 MB total per PR. Attachments can be deleted **before submission only**. | FR-PR-007 |
| BR-18 | Budget check is performed automatically on **submission** (not on save). If budget is exceeded, system behaviour is configurable per org policy (warn or block). | FR-PR-004 |

> ⚠️ **Discrepancy (BR-14):** BRD requires a warning when navigating away with unsaved changes. No such warning was observed — Cancel returns to the list immediately.

> ⚠️ **Discrepancy (BR-15):** Auto-save every 30s is specified in BRD. Not verified in current UI — URL stays at `/new` even after Save with no items, suggesting auto-save may not be active on the New form.

> ⚠️ **Discrepancy (BR-17):** No attachment UI was visible on the Create form. BRD specifies FR-PR-007 as 🚧 Pending — confirm if this is intentional.

---

## 2.9 Navigation

| Action | Destination |
|--------|-------------|
| **Cancel** | Step 1 — PR List |
| **Save** (with items) | Step 4 — PR Detail (Draft) at `/purchase-request/:uuid` |
| **Submit** → Confirm | Step 4 — PR Detail (INPROGRESS) |
| Breadcrumb **Purchase Request** | Step 1 — PR List |
